content: Just as human second-language learners use written reflexion (the vocabulary learning concept from high school English curricula) to solidify implicit word usage patterns by articulating how they reflect meaning in context, large language models can adapt a structured Self-Refine pipeline that incorporates this linguistic reflexion step to reduce hallucinations in domain-specific text generation. Reflexion on semantic alignment between output and target domain conventions acts as a human-aligned feedback anchor that improves the quality of iterative Self-Refine refinement.
